Run commands

Run commands actually control the course of simulation. Basically, you can tell the simulation to run for a particular amount of simulation time. Before and between these runs, you can schedule certain events, such as the creation of new molecules "by fiat" and dumping molecular populations and other simulation information to the console (stderr and stdout) or to a file.

  • seed - Seed random number generator.

  • echo - Print comments to output.

  • log - Print comments to log file.

  • create - Bring molecules into existence.

  • volume - Set the reaction volume.

  • grow - Periodically multiply or divide the volume by a factor.

  • run - Run for a given amount of simulation time.

  • dump - Dump simulation data (populations) periodically.

  • dump-once - Dump simulation data once.

  • dump-stats - Dump simulation data once with identifying tags.

  • trace - Generate output whenever a particular reaction happens.
